h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’d always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that wording made it sound as if there were no rule there at all.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to proceed directly to filling out the grammatical grid requested by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue analyzing how to translate the 0.4-second gain into operational capacity to compare against rollout costs.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the better frame is
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to directly answer the tutor's question about identifying the scar and the route on the rock face.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Now I think the correction is saying that is not really a belief about what happened, but more like a rule about who has to prove the case.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek clarification on the distinction between factual innocence and the legal presumption of innocence.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seeks to extend the trophic cascade model past plants to complete their exam answer.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that was the problem with my earlier way of saying it
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to directly answer the tutor's question about what would be left behind when the spoonful of water dries.
